
--- review at T175617641Z ---
_It appears @swagatpatel is working towards letting Plow agents complete real web browsing and authenticated website flows more reliably, without exposing the user’s 1Password secrets, by replacing the Camoufox Playwright endpoint with a persistent JSON-RPC browser service in `containers/browser/service.py`, adding the `plow-browser` wrapper and `fillSecret()` path in `containers/plow-starter/plow-browser/index.js`, and wiring Phoenix/Lima browser-state mounts plus 1Password `filter-logins`/`fetch-field` broker routes — reviewing against that goal._

**Overview** — This PR moves browsing onto a persistent RPC browser daemon with a 1Password broker path and Phoenix/Lima state wiring. The shape is directionally coherent, but the credential boundary is not yet tight enough to match the stated goal, and one shell bug currently prevents the starter from reaching the gateway.

**Strengths** — The browser readiness-file handoff is a good fail-loud improvement over silently starting agents without a browser endpoint. The `filter_logins` / `fillSecret` split is also the right privacy direction: selection metadata can stay agent-visible while plaintext stays server-side.

**Probes**

1. [blocking] [from: security] [bug] `fillSecret()` can fill a selected 1Password item into whatever page is active because `/internal/op/fetch-field` accepts only `item_id` and `field`, with no active-page URL/origin check. Files: `containers/browser/service.py:499`, `containers/browser/service.py:502`, `app/plowd/plowd/main.py:2502`, `app/plowd/plowd/main.py:2570`. Edit: Bind credential fetch to the active page origin: pass `page.url` from `containers/browser/service.py` into `/internal/op/fetch-field`, validate in `app/plowd/plowd/onepassword.py` that the 1Password item has a matching URL host before returning the field, and add focused tests for same-origin allow / cross-origin deny.

2. [blocking] [from: security] [bug] `browser.cookies()` exposes authenticated browser cookies directly to agent scripts, despite the new surface trying to keep bearer-equivalent credentials out of tool results. Files: `containers/browser/service.py:375`, `containers/browser/service.py:376`, `containers/plow-starter/plow-browser/index.js:109`, `app/openclaw/templates/workspace/skills/browser-usage/SKILL.md:67`. Edit: Delete the `cookies` verb from `containers/browser/service.py`, remove it from `containers/plow-starter/plow-browser/index.js`, and remove the skill example; net about -6 LOC plus test updates.

3. [blocking] [from: data-integrity] [bug] `containers/plow-starter/start.sh` runs under `/bin/sh` but uses bash-only `${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN:0:8}`, so the starter exits before the gateway `exec`. Files: `containers/plow-starter/start.sh:1`, `containers/plow-starter/start.sh:42`, `containers/plow-starter/Dockerfile:1`. Edit: In `containers/plow-starter/start.sh`, replace the bash-only substring with POSIX-safe prefix formatting, e.g. `token_prefix="$(printf '%.8s' "$OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN")"` and echo `${token_prefix}`; about +1/-1 LOC.

The PR does this by replacing the Playwright WS launch path with `containers/browser/service.py` plus the `plow-browser` JSON-RPC wrapper, mounting per-variant browser state via `AppPaths.browserStateDirectory()` and `/var/plow/browser-state`, and swapping `onepassword_fetch_credential` for `/internal/op/filter-logins`, `/internal/op/fetch-field`, and `browser.fillSecret(...)` — reviewing against that goal._

**Overview** — This re-review shows useful movement around route-level `filter_logins` normalization, but the main credential/session boundary blockers from the prior review are still present. The incremental patch also adds a second broker-side normalization contract and duplicate route tests, which moves against the repo’s pre-PMF bias toward one obvious seam.

--- review at T184025956Z ---
_It appears @swagatpatel is working towards letting users have Plow reliably handle authenticated browser workflows without repeatedly re-signing in or exposing credentials to the agent by replacing the old Camoufox launch path with a persistent JSON-RPC browser service in `containers/browser/service.py`, persisting per-variant browser profile/fingerprint state via the Phoenix/Lima `browser-state` mount, and routing 1Password lookups through `onepassword_filter_logins` plus `browser.fillSecret()` backed by plowd’s `/internal/op/fetch-field` broker path. — reviewing against that goal._

**Overview** — This re-review shows real progress: cookies are no longer exposed, and `/internal/op/fetch-field` now has broker-side origin validation. The remaining issues are concentrated around the credential fill boundary, plus a recurring duplicated-seam pattern that has survived multiple review rounds.

**Strengths** — Removing `cookies()` from both the service dispatch and wrapper is the right privacy posture. The strict host-equality check before broker reveal is also the right default for a password broker.

**Probes**

1. [blocking] [from: shape] [shape] The same parallel-seam class has recurred across 2 prior reviews plus this round: browser/plowd config still has injected `PLOWD_BASE_URL` beside a slot-0 fallback, 1Password selection still has `filter_logins` beside full-index `list_logins`, and the new patch adds route normalization beside broker normalization plus duplicate route-test homes. Files: `containers/browser/service.py:97`, `app/vm/provision-runtime.sh:279`, `app/openclaw/plugins/onepassword/index.js:80`, `app/openclaw/templates/workspace/skills/onepassword/SKILL.md:13`, `app/plowd/plowd/onepassword.py:409`, `app/plowd/tests/test_onepassword_endpoints.py:94`, `app/plowd/tests/test_plowd_contract.py:513`. Edit: Address the class instead of the instance: choose one browser config source, one credential-selection path, one normalization owner, and one route-contract test home. Without that move, expect another local-fix round on the next variant. Evidence: prior reviews T175617641Z probes 5/8 and T182418096Z probes 5/8/9/10 carried the same duplicated-seam shape; this round reintroduces it in shape probes 1-4 and consumers probe 2.

2. [blocking] [from: security] [bug] `fillSecret()` can still leak plaintext back to the agent if it fills a password into a non-password input: `form()` redacts only `type === "password"`, `dom()` only redacts `<input type="password" ... value="...">`, and `eval()` only blocks when a password-typed input has a value. Files: `containers/browser/service.py:523`, `containers/browser/service.py:526`, `containers/browser/service.py:369`, `containers/browser/service.py:417`, `containers/plow-starter/plow-browser/index.js:113`. Edit: In `containers/browser/service.py`, mark a page secret-tainted after non-username `fill_secret` and block agent-visible read verbs (`eval`, `dom`, `form`, `read`, `aria`, `find`) until navigation/page reset; add focused service tests for password filled into a text input.

3. [blocking] [from: data-integrity] [bug] The active page can navigate after the broker authorizes `page_url` but before `page.fill(...)`, so Touch ID or `op` latency can type a user’s secret into a different origin than the one plowd approved. Files: `containers/browser/service.py:523`, `containers/browser/service.py:524`, `containers/browser/service.py:526`, `app/plowd/plowd/onepassword.py:310`, `app/plowd/plowd/onepassword.py:329`. Edit: Re-read `page.url` immediately before `page.fill` and abort/retry through the broker if it differs from the URL that was authorized.

4. [blocking] [from: tests] [tests] The startup race fix in `containers/plow-starter/start.sh` has no shell-level regression coverage, so the route that keeps agents from starting without `CAMOUFOX_RPC_ENDPOINT` can regress silently. Files: `containers/plow-starter/start.sh:55`, `containers/plow-starter/start.sh:67`, `containers/plow-starter/start.sh:75`. Edit: Add release-script coverage that exercises timeout exit and ready-endpoint export.

5. [medium] [from: security] [bug] The browser daemon only needs broker fetch access, but it still receives the shared `plow-local-token` through a full `/config/secrets` mount, so browser-container compromise expands to every local-token route and token file in that directory. Files: `app/vm/provision-runtime.sh:290`, `containers/browser/service.py:98`, `app/plowd/plowd/main.py:752`. Edit: Add a broker-scoped token for `/internal/op/fetch-field` and mount only that token into the browser container.

6. [medium] [from: data-integrity] [bug] Any `_launch_and_serve()` exception still runs profile recovery and can wipe persisted browser session state even when the failure is a bind/server problem rather than profile corruption. Files: `containers/browser/service.py:758`, `containers/browser/service.py:781`, `containers/browser/service.py:788`, `containers/browser/service.py:662`. Edit: Stop running profile recovery for every launch exception; remove the catch-all wipe or narrow it to a specific profile-corruption failure.

7. [medium] [from: consumers] [simplification] The service and wrapper error names are out of sync: stale pages envelope as `ValueError: PageNotFound...`, and `OpOriginMismatch` falls through the wrapper as generic `BrowserRpcError`, while shipped skills tell agents to branch on typed names. Files: `containers/browser/service.py:266`, `containers/browser/service.py:550`, `containers/plow-starter/plow-browser/index.js:33`, `app/openclaw/templates/workspace/skills/browser-usage/SKILL.md:110`, `app/openclaw/templates/workspace/skills/onepassword/SKILL.md:102`. Edit: Align producer/consumer names by raising real service-side typed exceptions or teaching `_typedFromEnvelope` the actual envelopes.

8. [medium] [from: security] [simplification] Temporary `INSTRUMENT_AUTH` logging still prints bearer-token prefixes and lengths in normal runtime paths. Files: `app/plowd/plowd/gateway.py:311`, `app/plowd/plowd/gateway.py:383`, `app/plowd/plowd/gateway_operator.py:130`, `containers/plow-starter/start.sh:45`. Edit: Delete the `INSTRUMENT_AUTH` prefix/length log lines from plowd, gateway operator, and starter.

9. [medium] [from: performance] [perf] `/internal/op/*` routes are `async def` but call synchronous `op` subprocess paths, so a slow Touch ID / CLI call can block unrelated plowd requests on the event loop. Files: `app/plowd/plowd/main.py:2592`, `app/plowd/plowd/main.py:2601`, `app/plowd/plowd/onepassword.py:59`. Edit: Make the subprocess-backed handlers sync `def` handlers so FastAPI runs them in its threadpool.

**Security** — Blocking: `fillSecret()` can still expose plaintext through agent-visible read verbs, and its broker-approved origin can change before the actual fill.

**Test coverage** — Blocking: the fixed browser readiness startup race lacks regression coverage; `just test` failed later in plowd tests because this reviewer environment has no `/tmp`, which looks reviewer-side rather than PR-related.

> **Why this PR isn't converging?**
>
> This is now 11 review rounds, with additions moving from 5,590 to 7,612 (+2,022) and the LOC trend classified STABLE, while the recurring parallel-seam class is still driving the review. The structural ask has narrowed as browser config and list-vs-filter were removed, but since round 3 the PR has still not settled one owner for credential broker authorization, browser read/taint policy, route/error contracts, and readiness/mount behavior. Through the Broken-Glass Test, each push is still closing one exposed leaf by adding or reshaping another adjacent branch, which adds complexity and makes PMF iteration harder. Continuing this way calcifies the browser/credential boundary as a set of coupled exceptions instead of one obvious authenticated browsing path. Are we converging on a single ownership model for the remaining seams, or is the team choosing to keep paying this round-by-round complexity cost? Should we hold off on Findings 2-N until that structural direction is settled?

The remaining blockers are concentrated around broad login metadata discovery, taint lifecycle holes, and repeated parallel ownership around broker/readiness/test seams.

**Strengths** — The distinct broker-token auth dependency is the right privacy direction for keeping plaintext fetch-field access out of agent-visible code. The current fail-loud `PLOWD_BASE_URL` shape also removes one of the earlier silent fallback paths.
